frameworks.name: drift-diffusion model
frameworks.one_line: DDM extended with a learning rule over input weights from feedback

frameworks.name: Bayesian learning
frameworks.one_line: trial-by-trial update of weight mean and covariance

frameworks.name: confidence-modulated learning
frameworks.one_line: learning rate scales with decision confidence and correctness

core_question: How can a DDM learn near-optimal input weights from trial-by-trial feedback?
key_assumptions: sensory inputs encoded by large neuronal populations, not antineuron pairs
decision read out as a linear combination of population activity
weights treated as Bayesian variables with mean and covariance
manipulations: prior probability of each choice (asymmetric priors)
key_findings: derived a Bayesian learning rule for DDM input weights
learning rate proportional to confidence after errors, inversely after correct
in volatile environments rule predicts post-correct choice repetition bias
repetition-bias strength modulated by previous trial's difficulty
interpretations: confidence acts as a normative gain on weight updates during decision learning
history biases can emerge from optimal learning, not just heuristics
